     The Numbers Business Report

Hidden Figures (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $169,607,287Details
International Box Office $58,761,843
Worldwide Box Office $228,369,130
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $21,345,157
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $11,533,282
Total $32,878,439
Further financial details...

Synopsis

As the United States raced against Russia to put a man in space, NASA found untapped talent in a group of African-American female mathematicians that served as the brains behind one of the greatest operations in U.S. history. Based on the unbelievably true life stories of three of these women, known as “human computers”, we follow these women as they quickly rose the ranks of NASA alongside many of history’s greatest minds specifically tasked with calculating the momentous launch of astronaut John Glenn into orbit, and guaranteeing his safe return. Dorothy Vaughn, Mary Jackson, and Katherine Johnson crossed all gender, race, and professional lines while their brilliance and desire to dream big, beyond anything ever accomplished before by the human race, firmly cemented them in U.S. history as true American heroes.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$515,499 (0.3% of total gross)
Legs:7.44 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:74.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$25,000,000 (worldwide box office is 9.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:25 opening theaters/3,416 max. theaters, 8.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $226,241,053

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 25th, 2016 (Limited) by 20th Century Fox
January 6th, 2017 (Expands Wide) by 20th Century Fox
February 12th, 2021 (Limited) by Walt Disney
International Releases: January 20th, 2017 (Wide), released as Figuras ocultas (Spain)
January 23rd, 2017 (Wide) (South Korea)
January 27th, 2017 (Wide) (New Zealand)
January 27th, 2017 (Wide) (Taiwan)
February 3rd, 2017 (Wide) (Argentina)
Video Release: April 11th, 2017 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for thematic elements and some language.
(Rating bulletin 2448 (Cert #50479), 10/26/2016)
Running Time: 126 minutes
Award nomination: 2017 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Screen Actors Guild Outstanding Performance by a Cast in a Motion Picture Winner
Cast, crew, or production detail: Archive Footage, Epilogue, Intertitle
Plot point: African Americans, Bigotry, Biography, Mathematics, Sexism, Widow/Widower, Women’s Rights
Social setting: Civil Rights Movement, Space Program
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Time period setting: 1960s
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Fox 2000 Pictures, Chernin Entertainment, Levantine Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

As the United States raced against Russia to put a man in space, NASA found untapped talent in a group of African-American female mathematicians that served as the brains behind one of the greatest operations in U.S. history. Based on the unbelievably true life stories of three of these women, known as “human computers”, we follow these women as they quickly rose the ranks of NASA alongside many of history’s greatest minds specifically tasked with calculating the momentous launch of astronaut John Glenn into orbit, and guaranteeing his safe return. Dorothy Vaughn, Mary Jackson, and Katherine Johnson crossed all gender, race, and professional lines while their brilliance and desire to dream big, beyond anything ever accomplished before by the human race, firmly cemented them in U.S. history as true American heroes.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$515,499 (0.3% of total gross)
Legs:7.44 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:74.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$25,000,000 (worldwide box office is 9.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:25 opening theaters/3,416 max. theaters, 8.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $226,241,053

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 25th, 2016 (Limited) by 20th Century Fox
January 6th, 2017 (Expands Wide) by 20th Century Fox
February 12th, 2021 (Limited) by Walt Disney
International Releases: January 20th, 2017 (Wide), released as Figuras ocultas (Spain)
January 23rd, 2017 (Wide) (South Korea)
January 27th, 2017 (Wide) (New Zealand)
January 27th, 2017 (Wide) (Taiwan)
February 3rd, 2017 (Wide) (Argentina)
Video Release: April 11th, 2017 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for thematic elements and some language.
(Rating bulletin 2448 (Cert #50479), 10/26/2016)
Running Time: 126 minutes
Award nomination: 2017 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Screen Actors Guild Outstanding Performance by a Cast in a Motion Picture Winner
Cast, crew, or production detail: Archive Footage, Epilogue, Intertitle
Plot point: African Americans, Bigotry, Biography, Mathematics, Sexism, Widow/Widower, Women’s Rights
Social setting: Civil Rights Movement, Space Program
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Time period setting: 1960s
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Fox 2000 Pictures, Chernin Entertainment, Levantine Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
